title: Samsung Galaxy Z Fold8, Fold8 Ultra, Flip8, Watch9, Watch Ultra 2 prices leak
description: Samsung is widely expected to unveil the Galaxy Z Fold8, Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra, Galaxy Z Flip8, Galaxy Watch9, and Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 at an Unpacked event taking place in London on July 22. Ahead of that, today we have leaked European prices for the entire lineup. Spoiler alert: they're higher than last year. The Fold8 Ultra, which is the successor to the Fold7, will start at €2,199, €100 more than the Fold7. That's for 256GB of storage. Samsung Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra leaked image If you want 512GB, you'll have to pay €2,399, which is €180 more than for the Fold7. For the 1TB...

title: Insider claims that OxygenOS and Realme UI will be merged into ColorOS
description: Back in 2021, Pete Lau revealed that OxygenOS and ColorOS will merge. That meant that OnePlus phones would no longer have a separate software branch from Oppo phones. Now a knowledgeable insider claims that the company will bring things to their logical conclusion – merging Realme UI too. This way, the three related but previously independent brands will use the same software, ColorOS, which will help reduce R&D costs. But this is only part of a wider consolidation effort. We’ve already seen that OnePlus Germany is pushing users towards Oppo devices. And almost everything on OnePlus UK...

title: Oppo Reno16 review
Redmi 17 5G certifications reveal its RAM/storage options and battery capacity
description: The global Redmi 17 5G will be the successor to the Redmi 15 5G, as Redmi is skipping the number 16 just like Xiaomi did last year. Now, the Redmi 17 5G has been certified for sale in Malaysia by SIRIM and Taiwan by NCC. These certifications have revealed its model number, 26062RN92G, and the fact that it will be available in four RAM/storage configurations: 4/64GB, 4/128GB, 4/256GB, and 8/256GB. The phone will pack a 7,500 mAh battery with support for 45W fast charging. Redmi 15 5G The Redmi 17 5G will obviously support 5G, as well as dual-band Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, NFC, and GPS. It...

title: Report: the Xiaomi 18 series will be the first to launch with the new Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 6
description: Qualcomm’s annual Snapdragon Summit will take place between September 22 and 24. This will be the official unveiling of the next generation of Snapdragon 8 Elite chips (two of them) and several makers want to launch their flagship series that same month – Xiaomi, vivo and Oppo. The latest rumor claims that Xiaomi will get there first. The Xiaomi 18 series will consist of three models. The Xiaomi 18 Pro Max will have two 200MP cameras (including a 1/1.28” LOFIC sensor in the main). The Xiaomi 18 Pro will have a larger rear screen and possibly a Privacy Display feature. The Xiaomi 18 will be...

title: OnePlus devices are out of stock in another market
description: Things haven’t been looking great for OnePlus lately as the brand is in the midst of a massive restructuring across several markets. Earlier this week we reported on how OnePlus is actively pushing Oppo devices to potential customers in Europe, and we now have more evidence that the brand is likely done for in another key market. OnePlus smartphones out of stock on OnePlus UK While not entirely phased out just yet, most OnePlus devices are now out of stock on the company’s official UK webstore. This includes smartphones like the OnePlus 15, OnePlus 15R as well as tablets like the Pad...

title: Nothing Phone (1) receives final update as software support ends
description: The Nothing Phone (1) has officially reached the end of its software support lifecycle and will no longer receive software updates, the company confirmed in an official announcement. The smartphone debuted in July 2022 running Android 12. Nothing has started rolling out the final software update for the Phone (1), with the release expected to reach all users over the coming days. The update includes the July 2026 Android security patch, along with general bug fixes, performance improvements, and enhanced system stability. While the Phone (1) will no longer receive software...

title: HMD announces four new Nokia feature phones with AI
description: HMD is not giving up on feature phones, and its latest lineup of Nokia models is in with the times. The Nokia 200 4G, 210 4G, 215 4G 2nd Edition, and 235 4G 2nd Edition come with an on-device AI assistant which is accessible via the AI button placed front and center on their D-pad. HMD Nokia 200 4G The AI features are powered by Sikey AI and allow users to ask basic queries as well as control device functions via their voice. According to HMD’s spec pages, the AI assistant is free to use for the first 180 days but requires a paid subscription after. Interestingly enough, you’ll need...

title: Oppo Find X10 Ultra's main telephoto camera detailed in new leak
description: The first details about the successor to the Oppo Find X9 Ultra surfaced last month. Now, a fresh leak has revealed more information about the Oppo Find X10 Ultra, even though the flagship is still at least a year away from its expected debut. According to the leak, Oppo is testing a new 200MP periscope telephoto camera with a large 1/1.3-inch sensor for an upcoming flagship. While the device wasn't named, it is believed to be the Find X10 Ultra. Oppo Find X9 Ultra The leak also suggests that Oppo is evaluating another 200MP telephoto sensor with an even larger 1/1.12-inch sensor,...

title: vivo X500 tipped to feature a 50MP main rear camera
description: vivo is expected to unveil the vivo X500, X500 Pro, and the X500 Pro Max in China later this year, likely around September. Ahead of the rumored launch, new details about the base X500 rear camera setup have now surfaced online. According to tipster Digital Chat Station, the vivo X500 will ditch the 200MP primary camera found on its predecessor, the vivo X300. Instead, the upcoming flagship is tipped to feature a 50MP Sony sensor measuring 1/1.28 inches. vivo X300 offers a 200MP main rear camera The handset is also tipped to feature a 50MP ultrawide camera and a 64MP periscope...

title: Samsung Galaxy S27 Pro and Galaxy S27 Ultra to get a new selfie camera
description: Earlier today we found out that the entire Galaxy S27 family is rumored to have the Privacy Display feature that made its debut on the Galaxy S26 Ultra. Now, a new rumor brings some news about the Galaxy S27 Pro and Galaxy S27 Ultra, specifically their selfie camera. This will allegedly be a new 16MP one. The source of this information assumes that it might be a square sensor, emulating what Apple's done in the iPhone 17 series, but that's just speculation at the moment. The S27 and S27+ also getting the new sensor isn't entirely ruled out, but it's not clear whether that will happen just...

title: iQOO Z11i debuts with a 6,500mAh battery, Snapdragon 4 Gen 2 SoC
description: iQOO has quietly expanded its Z11 series in China with the launch of the new iQOO Z11i. The smartphone is powered by a Snapdragon chipset and packs a 6,500mAh battery. It features a 6.74-inch LCD display that offers a refresh rate of 120Hz and an HD+ resolution. The panel is claimed to reach up to 1,200 nits in high-brightness mode. The iQOO Z11i comes with a 6,500mAh battery and supports 15W wired charging. The handset runs Android 16-based OriginOS 6 out-of-the-box. It is powered by a Snapdragon 4 Gen 2 chipset, which is paired with up to 8GB of LPDDR4X RAM and up to 256GB of...

title: Motorola launches Global Connect eSIM app
description: Motorola has recently released a new Android app called Global Connect, in partnership with Gigs. It's available in the Google Play Store in Brazil, Mexico, Argentina, Peru, and Chile for now, but is scheduled to expand to several European markets later this year. The app also comes pre-installed on a lot of compatible Motorola devices. It offers eSIM data plans across the world, with various options available for different prices, with different data allowances included. To start you off, you get 1GB of complimentary data which can be used in 160 countries. Motorola Global...

title: Lenovo starts teasing a new Legion gaming tablet with 5G, here's when it's coming
description: Lenovo launched the Legion Y700 (Gen 5) tablet in March, and now the company is working on a new version. Currently teased as the Y700 Unlimited (machine translated from Chinese), it's going to be made official in August, and it will have 5G support. Until then, we can expect to see more teasers in the next few weeks. For now, we have the video you can see below, which showcases the new design of the camera island and its RGB LED ring. The Legion Y700 (Gen 5) has an 8.8-inch 1904x3040 LCD screen with a 165Hz refresh rate and 800-nit peak brightness, the Snapdragon 8...

title: Google loses final appeal at Europe's high court, to pay €4.1 billion fine
description: Google has lost its final appeal to the European Commission's €4.1 billion fine from 2018. The appeal was Google's last chance and was presented at the European Court of Justice (ECJ), Europe's highest court. The record-breaking fine sat at €4.34 (later reduced to €4.1) was imposed on Google by the European Union's executive arm, the European Commission, back in 2018. The EC found that Google was abusing Android's mobile dominance and giving its own apps an unfair advantage by requiring phone makers to pre-install Google Search, Chrome, and the Play Store. The EU sees it as Google's...

title: Instagram Edits adds support for bilingual captions, new templates
description: Instagram launched its CapCut rival Edits in January last year, and now the app has gained some new features. These include support for bilingual captions, which automatically translate your captions into a secondary language. Currently supported languages are English, Indonesian, Russian, Portuguese, Gujarati, Spanish, Hindi, Korean, Bengali, German, Italian, Thai, French, Japanese, and Kannada. Instagram Edits updates You can also build richer templates with support for overlays as well as the ability to lock specific clips. To round things off, you get more summer-related...

title: Amazon's Starlink competitor is almost ready
description: Amazon has been building up its own Low Earth Orbit satellite constellation for a while now, even calling it Amazon Leo (Low Earth Orbit, get it?). This is inevitably going to compete with SpaceX's Starlink, which is currently the largest satellite constellation. Today, Amazon's VP in charge of Leo, Chris Weber, has just announced that over 390 Leo satellites have been deployed already, which is apparently enough "to support continuous service across initial latitudes". He further states that the company has already completed "enough launches for initial service this year", strongly...

title: AT&T expands Build-A-Plan to include home internet
description: Back in May, AT&T launched Build-A-Plan, a new way to customize your wireless plan's perks and pricing. It seems to have been a success, as the company is now expanding the program to include home internet as well. The expansion is taking place on July 7. Starting on that day, if you use Build-A-Plan you will be able to add home internet to your plan as well - either AT&T Fiber if it’s available in your area, or, if it’s not, AT&T Internet Air, which basically gives you fixed internet access by using AT&T's mobile network. If you use Build-A-Plan and want the cheapest possible...

title: Foldable iPhone Ultra production target reportedly climbs to 10 million units
description: The foldable iPhone, rumored to be called the iPhone Ultra, is expected to debut alongside the iPhone 18 Pro series this September. Ahead of its anticipated launch, a new report claims Apple has placed a production order for around 10 million units. According to a Nikkei Asia report citing sources familiar with the matter, Apple has asked its suppliers to manufacture around 10 million foldable iPhones in 2026. Notably, earlier reports suggested that the company was targeting an initial production run of 7 to 8 million units. The report claims that Apple has asked suppliers to...

title: Samsung Galaxy Glasses emerge in new leaked video
description: We’ve already seen leaked renders and additional details about Samsung’s Galaxy Glasses. We now have our best look yet thanks to an official-looking video shared by the folks over at SamMobile. The 27-second clip goes over the key design elements of the Galaxy Glasses, including their square lenses and touch-sensitive area on the right temple. We can also spot an LED indicator on the right and a camera on the other side. The video also showcases their power button which will be placed on the right temple. While we don’t get additional details, this is one of our best looks yet...
